clinical_history: What is this patient's chief complaint and what is the cause?
diagnosis: Probably pleuritic-type chest pain with ECG pattern of acute pericarditis (diffuse ST elevations and subtle PR segment shifts--PR up in aVR and down inferior-laterally, opposite of ST segment). Sinus tachycardia with pericarditis pattern may be due to a number of factors including a) pain; b) pericardial effusion/tamponade; c) associated myocarditis; d) fever.
